message:
feat(release): Add release verify subcommand and improve upload reliability (bd-1jt.3.10)

- Add `dsr release verify <tool> <version>` command for release validation
- Verify assets against build manifest, check for missing/extra files
- Optional --verify-checksums flag for SHA256 spot-checks
- Optional --fix flag to re-upload missing assets
- Fix build_retry_with_backoff exit code capture (was using $? incorrectly)
- Improve gh_upload_asset to use curl directly with proper token handling
- Remove unused local variable in cmd_build

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-130","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage","description":"# EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage

## Goal
Achieve full unit test coverage for all source modules plus E2E integration tests for all CLI commands, without using mocks/fakes where possible - testing real behavior.

## Current State
- **13 source modules** in src/
- **7 have unit tests**: act_runner, build_state, git_ops, guardrails, host_health, logging, secrets
- **6 need unit tests**: config, github, host_selector, quality_gates, signing, toolchain_detect

## Test Philosophy
1. **Real behavior testing** - Test actual module functions, not mocked behavior
2. **Temporary directories** - Use mktemp for isolation, not production paths
3. **No external service mocking** - For github.sh, test validation logic; skip live API calls with skip markers
4. **Comprehensive edge cases** - Happy path, error paths, boundary conditions
5. **Detailed logging** - Every test outputs clear pass/fail with context

## Deliverables

### Unit Tests (Per Module)
Each module test should cover:
- All exported functions
- Input validation and error handling
- Edge cases and boundary conditions
- Return value verification
- Side effect verification (file creation, state changes)

### E2E Integration Tests
Each command test should cover:
- Successful execution path
- JSON mode output validation against schemas
- Error handling and exit codes
- Flag combinations
- XDG directory compliance

### Test Infrastructure
- Unified test runner script
- Structured logging for CI visibility
- Coverage metrics (function/line coverage estimation)
- Test result aggregation and reporting

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 modules have unit tests
- [ ] All implemented CLI commands have E2E tests
- [ ] Test runner executes all tests with summary
- [ ] Tests use real behavior, not mocks
- [ ] All tests are self-cleaning (no leftover files)
- [ ] Test output includes timing and detailed results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:16.745219090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:46:16.689874761Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:46:16.689425475Z","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: Theme 5 (bd-1jt.5) already serves as the testing epic. Unit test beads kept as standalone tasks.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1gm","title":"Unit tests for signing.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for signing.sh Module

## Module Overview
signing.sh provides minisign key management and artifact signing:
- `signing_check` - Verify keypair exists
- `signing_init` - Generate new keypair
- `signing_sign` - Sign an artifact
- `signing_verify` - Verify signature

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Key Generation Tests
- `signing_init` creates private key at correct path
- `signing_init` creates public key at correct path
- Private key has correct permissions (600)
- `signing_init --force` overwrites existing keys
- `signing_init` without --force refuses to overwrite
- `signing_init --no-password` creates unprotected key

### 2. Key Checking Tests
- `signing_check` succeeds when both keys exist
- `signing_check` fails when private key missing
- `signing_check` fails when public key missing
- `signing_check --json` returns structured status

### 3. Signing Tests
- `signing_sign <file>` creates .minisig file
- Signature file matches artifact name + .minisig
- Signing non-existent file produces error
- Signing without private key produces error
- Custom key path via --key flag

### 4. Verification Tests
- `signing_verify <file>` validates signature
- Verification fails with wrong public key
- Verification fails with tampered artifact
- Verification fails with missing signature
- Custom public key path via --key flag

### 5. Path Resolution Tests
- Default paths use XDG_CONFIG_HOME/dsr/secrets/
- Custom paths work correctly
- Relative paths resolved correctly

## Test Approach
- Create temp directory for keys and artifacts
- Generate real minisign keys for testing
- Sign and verify real files
- NO mocking minisign binary
- Clean up all temp files","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:59.824446424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:44:23.331040659Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:44:23.330651526Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: test_signing.sh already exists","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gm","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:59.824446424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt","title":"EPIC: Fallback Release Infrastructure (ACFS)","description":"# Agent-Controlled Fallback System (ACFS) for Multi-Tool Releases

## Background & Problem Statement

GitHub Actions is limiting usage due to high volume (~$5k/month equivalent). When CI/CD jobs stay queued for >10 minutes, it indicates throttling. We need an automated fallback system that:

1. **Detects throttling** - Monitors GH Actions queue time, triggers fallback when jobs sit >10 min
2. **Builds locally** - Linux on Threadripper workstation (trj), macOS on Mac mini (mmini)
3. **Handles Windows** - Cross-compilation or other strategies TBD
4. **Uploads releases** - Manually create releases and upload binaries when throttled
5. **Smart installers** - curl|bash one-liners that try release first, build from source if stale

## Infrastructure Available

| Host | Alias | Purpose | Connection |
|------|-------|---------|------------|
| Threadripper Workstation | trj | Linux x86_64/ARM64 builds | local |
| Mac mini | mmini | macOS ARM64/x86_64 builds | ssh mmini |
| OVH servers | yto, fmd | Backup build capacity | ssh yto/fmd |

## Tools Affected (16 total from Dicklesworthstone toolchain)

cass, bv, cm, ubs, xf, ru, slb, caam, ntm, br, dcg, ms, wa, pt, rch, mcp_agent_mail

## Languages & Toolchains

- **Rust** (majority): cargo, rustup, cross-compilation via cargo-zigbuild or cross
- **Go**: go build, goreleaser  
- **TypeScript/Bun**: bun build, bun compile

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Any tool can be released within 30 minutes even when GH Actions is throttled
2. curl-bash installers work on Linux/macOS/Windows without manual intervention
3. Installers auto-detect stale releases (>10 commits behind) and offer source builds
4. Toolchain installation is automatic and non-destructive
5. All paths have unit tests and E2E validation scripts with detailed logging

## Key Design Decisions

1. **10-minute threshold**: Chosen as balance between patience and productivity
2. **Try release first**: Network download is faster than compilation for most users
3. **Commit-based staleness**: More accurate than date-based (active development = fresh releases)
4. **Non-destructive toolchains**: Never break existing installations  
5. **Detailed logging**: Every step logged for debugging failed installs

## Architecture Overview

\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ACFS Release Pipeline                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [1] Push tag v1.2.3                                                │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [2] GH Actions triggered                                           │
│         │                                                            │
│         ├──→ Queued <10min? ──→ Build & Release (normal path)       │
│         │                                                            │
│         └──→ Queued >10min? ──→ ACFS Fallback Triggered             │
│                    │                                                 │
│                    ▼                                                 │
│              [3] Local Builds                                       │
│                    │                                                 │
│         ┌─────────┴─────────┬────────────────┐                      │
│         ▼                   ▼                ▼                      │
│    Linux (trj)        macOS (mmini)    Windows (cross)              │
│         │                   │                │                      │
│         └─────────┬─────────┴────────────────┘                      │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│              [4] Upload binaries to GitHub Release                  │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│              [5] Update Homebrew/Scoop formulas                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 Smart Curl-Bash Installer Flow                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  curl -sSL install.sh | bash                                        │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [1] Detect OS/Arch                                                 │
│         │                                                            │
│         ▼                                                            │
│  [2] Check latest release                                           │
│         │                                                            │
│         ├──→ Release exists & fresh (<10 commits behind)?           │
│         │         │                                                  │
│         │         └──→ Download binary ──→ Install ──→ Done         │
│         │                                                            │
│         └──→ Release missing or stale?                              │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [3] Prompt: "Build from source?"                            │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [4] Check/Install toolchain (Rust/Go/Bun)                   │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [5] Clone repo & build                                      │
│                   │                                                  │
│                   ▼                                                  │
│         [6] Install binary ──→ Done                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

## Dependency Graph (high-level themes)

1. **Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring** - No deps, can start immediately
2. **Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure** - Needs monitoring to know when to trigger
3. **Theme 3: Release Upload Automation** - Needs builds to have artifacts to upload
4. **Theme 4: Smart Installers** - Can be developed in parallel, integrates with releases
5. **Theme 5: Testing & Validation** - Depends on all above being implemented

## Success Criteria
- [ ] End-to-end fallback pipeline works for all tools
- [ ] Installers detect stale releases and offer source builds
- [ ] Cross-platform builds + releases verified
- [ ] Unit/integration/E2E tests pass with detailed logs

-{"id":"bd-1jt.1","title":"Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring & Timeout Detection","description":"# Theme 1: GH Actions Monitoring & Timeout Detection

## Purpose
Detect when GH Actions runs are throttled (queued >10 minutes) and automatically trigger the fallback build system.

## Rationale
- GH Actions throttling manifests as prolonged queue time
- We need **deterministic** detection using structured API data (no human output parsing)
- 10-minute threshold balances patience with productivity

## Implementation: Use GitHub REST API JSON
Use the workflow runs endpoint and compute queue time from timestamps:
```bash
json=$(gh_api "repos/$owner/$repo/actions/runs?per_page=20")
queued=$(echo "$json" | jq -r '.workflow_runs[] | select(.status=="queued") | .created_at')
```

## Key Components
1. **dsr check** - Check if GH Actions is throttled for a repo
2. **dsr watch** - Continuously monitor and trigger fallback when needed
3. **dsr status** - Show current queue status across all repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Can detect a queued run within 1 minute of hitting 10min mark
- [ ] False positive rate <1%
- [ ] Works across all 16 tools' repositories
- [ ] Clear logging of detection and trigger events
- [ ] Exit codes: 0=OK, 1=throttled, 3=dependency/auth, 8=network

## Dependencies
- gh CLI (authenticated) OR GITHUB_TOKEN
- jq for JSON parsing
- date command with GNU extensions (or macOS-compatible fallback)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:15.526804311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:08.469458787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:15.526804311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.1","title":"Implement dsr check command - detect throttled runs","description":"# Implement `dsr check` — Detect Throttled GH Actions Runs

## Purpose
Detect when GitHub Actions is throttled (runs stuck in queue) and provide structured data for automation.

## Data Source (GitHub REST API)
Use workflow runs JSON from `repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` (via `gh api` or curl fallback). Avoid parsing human output.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr check <repo>
dsr check --all
 dsr check <repo> --threshold 10
 dsr check <repo> --json
```

## Detection Logic (robust)
1. Resolve short repo → full `owner/repo`
2. Fetch latest workflow runs via `gh_api` wrapper (with caching)
3. **Queued throttling**:
   - `status == "queued"` and `now - created_at > threshold`
4. **Stuck in_progress**:
   - `status == "in_progress"` and (`run_started_at == null` OR `now - run_started_at > threshold`)
5. Include `event`, `workflow_id`, `head_sha`, `html_url` for context

## Output (JSON)
```json
{
  "repo": "Dicklesworthstone/ntm",
  "status": "throttled",
  "oldest_queued_min": 15,
  "threshold_min": 10,
  "runs": [
    {"id":12345,"status":"queued","created_at":"...","workflow_id":999}
  ]
}
```

## Error Handling
- Auth missing → exit 3 (dependency/auth)
- Rate limit/network → exit 8 (network)
- Invalid args → exit 4

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly flags queued/stuck runs using API timestamps
- [ ] JSON output includes run IDs + workflow IDs + html_url
- [ ] Uses cached responses (short TTL)
- [ ] Works across all configured repos","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:35.923073198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:54:26.983516350Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:54:26.983498096Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr check command with throttle detection. Works with JSON mode. ShellCheck clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:35.923073198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:00.946605637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:11.895264009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:52.255711563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:48.449588674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.2","title":"Implement dsr watch command - continuous monitoring daemon","description":"# Implement `dsr watch` — Continuous Monitoring Daemon

## Purpose
Continuously monitor all configured repos and trigger fallback only when throttling is real, stable, and deduped.

## Design Requirements
- **Jittered polling** to avoid thundering herds
- **Debounce** repeated throttling signals (avoid flapping)
- **Deduplicate** by run ID so each stuck run triggers at most once
- **Resilient** to temporary API failures (backoff + retry)

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr watch
 dsr watch --daemon
 dsr watch --interval 60
 dsr watch --dry-run
 dsr watch --once
```

## Implementation Notes
- Reuse `dsr check --json`
- Track triggered run IDs in `~/.local/state/dsr/triggered.json`
- Use a lightweight lockfile to avoid multiple watcher instances
- On startup, run `dsr doctor --quick`
- Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT to persist state + release lock

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Polls all repos at configured interval
- [ ] Triggers fallback once per throttled run
- [ ] Exponential backoff on API errors
- [ ] Daemon mode survives restarts and logs state
- [ ] --once performs a single check + exit","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:49.072822562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:13:20.819643910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:13:20.819625536Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete - cmd_watch with: jittered polling, debouncing, run deduplication, exponential backoff on API errors, triggered.json state tracking, lock file, --once/--daemon/--dry-run modes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:07:49.072822562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:20.660721260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:01.213534509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:12.167502295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:52.515880942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:51:52.647890515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.3","title":"Implement dsr repos command - manage tool registry","description":"# Implement dsr repos command — manage tool registry

## Purpose
Manage the repo registry used by build/release/install. This is the authoritative source for targets, commands, and workflow mappings.

## Registry Layout (XDG)
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/<tool>.yaml` (one file per tool)
- `schema_version: "1.0.0"`
- `repo`, `local_path`, `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets`, `workflow`, `act_job_map`, `checks`
- `artifact_naming`, `archive_format`, `enabled`

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr repos list
 dsr repos add <tool>
 dsr repos remove <tool>
 dsr repos info <tool> [--json]
 dsr repos validate
 dsr repos discover [--apply]
```

## Behavior
- Reads/writes `repos.d/` (merge all tool files deterministically)
- Validates required fields (repo, build_cmd, targets, workflow jobs)
- `discover` scans `/data/projects` for Cargo.toml/go.mod/package.json
- Discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never creates new directories; discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never uses global `cd` for git ops; use `git -C <path>`
- Uses `yq` when available for safe edits; otherwise read-only fallback

## Best-Practice Notes
- Normalize paths (absolute or `~`), reject relative
- Use guardrails for any file deletion (`repos remove`)
- stdout for JSON/paths, stderr for human output only

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List shows all tools with language + targets
- [ ] Add/remove works and persists per-tool files
- [ ] Info shows complete build config
- [ ] Validation catches missing fields
- [ ] Discover finds repos without mutating config unless confirmed
- [ ] Registry uses `repos.d` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:02.877692893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:07:51.168912408Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:06:16.702769732Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr repos command with list, add, remove, info, validate, discover, and sync subcommands. All ShellCheck warnings addressed. JSON schema validated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:02.877692893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:01.477484654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:52.755481675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:50.134696217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:47.636700054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.6","title":"Hybrid build strategy: act + SSH for cross-platform","description":"# Hybrid Build Strategy: act + Native Hosts

## Purpose
Combine `act` (Linux builds on trj) with native macOS + Windows hosts for reliable cross-platform artifacts.

## Constraints
- `act` runs Linux containers only
- macOS/Windows require native hosts (mmini, wlap)

## Orchestration Principles
- No `set -e`; capture exit codes per host
- No global `cd` for git ops; use `git -C` where possible
- Use `run_id` + state file to track host outcomes and resume safely
- Always emit per-host logs + manifest entries

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Linux builds via act on trj
- [ ] macOS builds via mmini
- [ ] Windows builds via wlap
- [ ] Partial failures reported clearly (no silent exits)
- [ ] Manifest + per-host logs written for every target","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:47.033880170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:30:18.192955606Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:30:18.192615005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:47.033880170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:36.739089034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.7","title":"Setup Windows build environment on Windows laptop (wlap)","description":"# Setup Windows Build Environment on Windows Laptop

## Purpose
Enable native Windows builds via SSH to a Windows laptop, avoiding cross-compilation limitations and ensuring native binary quality.

## Why Native Windows Builds?

Cross-compilation from Linux has limitations:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild works but can miss Windows-specific optimizations
- Go: Cross-compile works well but some CGO dependencies fail
- Bun: Limited cross-compile support

Native Windows builds provide:
- Proper Windows SDK integration
- Native MSVC optimizations
- Accurate testing environment
- Full Windows-specific feature support

## Prerequisites on Windows Laptop

### 1. Enable SSH Server
\`\`\`powershell
# Run as Administrator
Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0
Start-Service sshd
Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType 'Automatic'

# Allow through firewall
New-NetFirewallRule -Name 'SSH' -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22
\`\`\`

### 2. Configure SSH Key Auth
\`\`\`powershell
# Create .ssh directory
mkdir ~\.ssh

# Add authorized keys (copy from trj)
notepad ~\.ssh\authorized_keys
# Paste the public key from trj
\`\`\`

### 3. Install Build Toolchains

#### Rust
\`\`\`powershell
# Download and run rustup-init.exe
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri https://win.rustup.rs -OutFile rustup-init.exe
.\rustup-init.exe -y --default-toolchain nightly
# Restart shell
\`\`\`

#### Go
\`\`\`powershell
# Download from go.dev or use winget
winget install GoLang.Go
# Or chocolatey
choco install golang
\`\`\`

#### Bun
\`\`\`powershell
powershell -c "irm bun.sh/install.ps1 | iex"
\`\`\`

#### Visual Studio Build Tools (for Rust MSVC)
\`\`\`powershell
# Download Visual Studio Build Tools
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
# Select "Desktop development with C++" workload
\`\`\`

## SSH Configuration on trj/mmini

Add to ~/.ssh/config:
\`\`\`
Host wlap
    HostName <windows-laptop-ip>
    User <username>
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519
    # May need PowerShell as default shell
    # RemoteCommand powershell.exe
\`\`\`

Generate key:
\`\`\`bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519 -N ""
ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/wlap_ed25519 wlap
\`\`\`

## Build Commands via SSH

### Rust
\`\`\`bash
ssh wlap "cd C:\projects\cass && cargo build --release"
scp wlap:C:\projects\cass\target\release\cass.exe ./builds/
\`\`\`

### Go
\`\`\`bash
ssh wlap "cd C:\projects\ntm && go build -o ntm.exe ./cmd/ntm"
scp wlap:C:\projects\ntm\ntm.exe ./builds/
\`\`\`

## Updated Build Matrix

| Target | Host | Method |
|--------|------|--------|
| linux-amd64 | trj | native |
| linux-arm64 | trj | cross-compile |
| darwin-arm64 | mmini | native |
| darwin-amd64 | mmini | native |
| windows-amd64 | wlap | native |

## Verification Script
\`\`\`powershell
# C:\bin\dsr-verify-toolchains.ps1
Write-Host "=== DSR Toolchain Verification (Windows) ==="
Write-Host "Rust: " -NoNewline; rustc --version
Write-Host "Cargo: " -NoNewline; cargo --version
Write-Host "Go: " -NoNewline; go version
Write-Host "Bun: " -NoNewline; bun --version
Write-Host "MSVC: " -NoNewline; cl 2>&1 | Select-String "Version"
Write-Host "=== Done ==="
\`\`\`

## Networking Considerations
- If laptop is on different network, may need VPN or Tailscale
- Consider Tailscale for consistent IP across networks
- Test SSH from trj before relying on it

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH from trj to wlap works
- [ ] Rust builds produce valid .exe
- [ ] Go builds produce valid .exe
- [ ] File transfer (scp) works
- [ ] Verification script passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:16:39.813118424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:48:38.461156122Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:48:38.460880883Z","close_reason":"Partial: SSH, Go builds, SCP all work. Rust needs MSVC Build Tools (~6GB manual install). Go (1.25.6) and Bun (1.3.8) are ready. Rust (1.93.0) installed but MSVC linker missing - requires 'winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools' with C++ workload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:16:39.813118424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.8","title":"Implement host health checking before builds","description":"# Host Health Checking Before Builds

## Purpose
Fail fast if a host is offline or missing required capabilities. Avoid long SSH hangs and late build failures.

## Checks
- SSH connectivity (short timeout + BatchMode)
- Disk space threshold
- Toolchains (rust/go/bun)
- Docker/Colima for act (if Linux builds run on host)
- Host clock drift (optional, warn only)

## Output
- JSON summary for automation
- Cache results for short TTL (5m)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH check completes quickly
- [ ] Toolchains verified per host capabilities
- [ ] Clear warnings for low disk or missing deps
- [ ] Build uses only healthy hosts unless overridden","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:11.872176622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107460331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107442968Z","close_reason":"Integrated host_health.sh module into dsr CLI. Added health command with check/all/clear-cache subcommands. Checks SSH connectivity, disk space, toolchains, Docker status, and clock drift. Caches results for 5 minutes. Requires yq for host config lookups.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:11.872176622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.9","title":"Implement retry and recovery logic for failed builds","description":"# Retry and Recovery Logic

## Purpose
Handle transient failures gracefully without losing state or corrupting artifacts.

## Strategy
- Exponential backoff + jitter (avoid thundering herd)
- Only retry idempotent steps (ssh, download, upload)
- Persist state to resume after process crash

## Integration
- Uses build lock/state machine (bd-1jt.2.11)
- Skips completed hosts on resume
- Partial failures do not block successful hosts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Transient failures auto-recover
- [ ] State tracked persistently
- [ ] --resume works correctly
- [ ] Clear error reporting with logs","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:53.592266937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:25:25.450274351Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:53.592266937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.971484244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:16.822551477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3","title":"Theme 3: Release Upload Automation","description":"# Release Upload Automation

## Purpose
After local builds complete, create GitHub releases and upload binaries with proper checksums and signatures.

## The dsr release Command
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>           # Create release, upload all builds
dsr release <tool> <version> --draft   # Create as draft first
dsr release <tool> <version> --notes "..." # Custom release notes
```

## Release Workflow

### 1. Verify Builds Exist
```bash
# Check all expected artifacts exist
build_dir="$DSR_STATE_DIR/builds/$tool/$version"
for target in linux-amd64 linux-arm64 darwin-arm64 darwin-amd64 windows-amd64; do
  artifact="$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-${target}"
  [ -f "$artifact" ] || die "Missing: $artifact"
done
```

### 2. Generate Checksums (No global cd)
```bash
# Generate checksums in place
sha256sum "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-* > "$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-checksums.sha256"
```

### 3. Sign Artifacts (Optional but Recommended)
```bash
# Using minisign
for f in "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*; do
  minisign -Sm "$f" -s ~/.minisign/key.sec
done
```

### 4. Create GitHub Release
```bash
gh release create "$version" \
  --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" \
  --title "$tool $version" \
  --notes "$release_notes" \
  "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*
```

### 5. Verify Upload
```bash
gh release view "$version" --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" --json assets
```

## Release Notes Generation (Git Plumbing)
```bash
repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
last_tag=$(git -C "$repo_dir" describe --tags --abbrev=0 HEAD^)
git -C "$repo_dir" log --oneline ${last_tag}..HEAD > "$build_dir/release_notes.txt"
```

## Asset Naming Convention
```
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]
{tool}-{version}-checksums.sha256
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}.minisig
```

Examples:
```
ntm-v1.5.1-linux-amd64
ntm-v1.5.1-darwin-arm64
ntm-v1.5.1-windows-amd64.exe
ntm-v1.5.1-checksums.sha256
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates release with correct tag
- [ ] All binaries uploaded and downloadable
- [ ] Checksums file included
- [ ] Release notes auto-generated if not provided
- [ ] Works for all 16 tools

## Error Handling
- Release already exists → Prompt to update or abort
- Upload fails → Retry, then report which assets failed
- Tag doesn't exist → Create tag or error","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:45.249464757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.3.1","title":"Implement dsr release command - upload artifacts to GitHub","description":"# Implement `dsr release` — Upload Artifacts to GitHub

## Purpose
Create a GitHub Release from locally built artifacts, with retries, verification, and consistent metadata.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --artifacts <dir>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --draft
 dsr release <tool> <version> --generate-notes
 dsr release <tool> <version> --verify-tag
 dsr release <tool> <version> --dry-run
 dsr release <tool> <version> --resume
```

## Required Inputs
- Build manifest (source of truth)
- Checksums + signatures
- Optional SBOM + provenance

## Release Flow
1. Validate manifest + checksums
2. Create release tag (verify exists)
3. Upload binaries + checksums + signatures
4. Upload SBOM + provenance if configured
5. Verify assets match manifest

## Idempotency / Resume
- Safe to re-run (`gh release upload --clobber`)
- Track partial uploads in state for resume

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Release created with correct tag & notes
- [ ] All assets uploaded and verified
- [ ] Works for all tools
- [ ] JSON report includes asset list + validation results
- [ ] Resume re-uploads only missing/failed  {"id":"bd-1jt.3.10","title":"Implement release verification + retry/resume uploads","description":"# Implement Release Verification + Retry/Resume Uploads

## Purpose
Ensure GitHub releases contain the exact expected assets, and recover safely from partial upload failures.

## Verification Steps
- Compare release assets against build manifest
- Verify checksums.sha256 matches uploaded binaries
- Optionally re-download and verify sha256 for spot checks

## Retry Strategy
- Use `gh release upload --clobber` for failed assets
- Track failed uploads in state file for resume
- Idempotent uploads (safe to re-run)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing assets detected and reported
- [ ] Partial uploads can be resumed safely
- [ ] Release verification produces JSON report
- [ ] Re-running verification does not create duplicate assets","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:31:30.726550981Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:12.415821448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.651659081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:40.580783022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.11","title":"Add pre-release quality gates (typecheck/lint/test) per tool","description":"# Add Pre-Release Quality Gates (typecheck/lint/test) per Tool

## Purpose
Ensure every release passes the tool's own quality checks before upload to GitHub.

## Behavior
- Read `checks` from `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Run checks in deterministic order
- Support `--skip-checks` and `--dry-run`
- Log per-check duration + exit code
- Fail release if any check fails (exit 1/6 depending on stage)

## Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout (JSON): list of checks, durations, exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checks run per-tool before release upload
- [ ] Failures block release with clear errors
- [ ] JSON output includes per-check results
- [ ] Dry-run shows planned checks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:27:28.402142395Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:27:28.402125253Z","close_reason":"Implemented src/quality_gates.sh module with qg_run_checks() and qg_get_checks(). Added 'dsr quality' command with --dry-run, --skip-checks, --work-dir options. Reads checks from repos.yaml, logs per-check results with durations, returns JSON with passed/failed counts. Exit 1 on failure.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:21.256627491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:47.402939311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.2","title":"Update Homebrew and Scoop formulas after release","description":"# Update Homebrew and Scoop Formulas After Release

## Purpose
Update package manager metadata so users can install via `brew` and `scoop` with correct URLs + SHA256.

## Homebrew
- Update formula in `homebrew-tap`
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp` (never `/data/projects`)
- Validate with `brew audit --strict` and `brew style` when available
- Respect `--dry-run` (plan only)

## Scoop
- Update manifest JSON in `scoop-bucket`
- Use `sha256:<hex>` format
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp`
- Validate JSON and run `scoop checkver` when available

## Logging / Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout: JSON (when `--json`) listing updated formula/manifest paths + checksums

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Formula/manifest updated with correct version + hashes
- [ ] Dry-run available and deterministic
- [ ] Validation commands executed when available
